title: Hosted Codex Live OAuth Storage Tasks
principles: Gateway remains the default Eve model path until hosted live Codex mode has encrypted storage, refresh coordination, deployment protection, and rollback.
Use Effect Schema, tagged errors, Context services, Layers, Config, Redacted, and KeyValueStore for every runtime-owned boundary.
Hosted KV must store only encrypted Codex OAuth profiles, never raw access tokens, refresh tokens, authorization codes, or full OAuth payloads.
Treat Codex OAuth as a Codex-specific subscription runtime path, not an OpenAI Platform API key or Vercel AI Gateway credential.
Keep apps/agent out of Codex OAuth internals; the agent only selects Gateway or a private OpenAI-compatible proxy LanguageModel.
Do not adopt code from OpenClaw, Goose, Hermes, or Pi. Use them only as research precedent when explicitly cited.
globalVerification: Revalidate the current Codex OAuth surface and local Codex CLI behavior before implementing hosted OAuth exchange.
bun run --filter @bundjil/agent test when agent config or examples change
bun run --filter @bundjil/agent build when agent config changes
Vercel preview deploy under Cooper's personal Vercel account before production
Sanitized hosted OAuth proof with no tokens, codes, prompts, or raw OAuth payloads in logs or docs
Sanitized hosted live model proof with HTTP statuses, mode, content type, SSE line count, done marker, and leak booleans only
Upstash readback proof that stored profile values are encrypted and key-prefixed
Dedicated 3-pass Effect TS audit sweep task completed after implementation and documentation

implementationImprovementAuditCounter.description: Before accepting each task, run parent audit passes for ownership/call graph, Effect implementation quality, and verification coverage. Require more passes if OAuth support, encryption, lock semantics, Vercel deployment, or secret handling remains weak.

tasks.title: Revalidate Codex OAuth and local CLI login
tasks.description: Resolve or explicitly bypass the local Codex duplicate-install/login issue, then confirm the current OAuth issuer, client, scopes, PKCE, redirect, and token response shape without recording secrets.
implementationNotes: Inspect `which -a codex` and `codex --version` before relying on managed login behavior.
Record whether the active binary is the expected current Codex CLI or the app-bundled CLI.
Verify the localhost callback failure from 2026-07-07 is resolved or document why hosted OAuth can proceed without it.
Use current official docs, executor personal research, DeepWiki, or direct local inspection as needed, but record only sanitized findings.
Do not print, commit, or snapshot access tokens, refresh tokens, auth codes, raw OAuth responses, cookies, or account secrets.

2026-07-11 tracer bullet: the shell-selected standalone CLI at ~/.local/bin/codex reports 0.136.0 and `codex login status` reports a ChatGPT login. The pnpm shim reports 0.142.0 and the ChatGPT-app bundled binary reports 0.144.0-alpha.4; manual research is pinned to the selected standalone CLI. Only auth-cache field names were inspected.

2026-07-11 Executor Personal DeepWiki source research on openai/codex confirms the account-login issuer category, fixed public CLI client category, documented scope names, S256 PKCE, localhost callback behavior, and token-field names without recording secret values. It also confirms mcp_oauth_callback_url applies to remote MCP-server OAuth, not ChatGPT account login.

2026-07-11 decision: encryption, schema, storage, and lock tasks are unblocked. CodexOAuthClientLive, operator OAuth start/callback routes, and hosted live proof are blocked/deferred pending a supported OpenAI hosted grant with a registered Vercel redirect URI or a separately specified account-link mechanism.
mandatoryVerification: Codex CLI binary paths and versions are recorded in sanitized form.
OAuth issuer, client id category, scopes, redirect behavior, PKCE behavior, and token response fields are confirmed or implementation is blocked.
The hosted redirect URI strategy is explicitly accepted, blocked, or deferred with evidence.
Flat meaningful Effect.gen programs are explicitly required for the implementation tasks that follow.
Typed errors handled in .pipe(...) with catchTag, catchTags, or mapError are explicitly required.
Canonical Effect Schema contracts and schema-derived types from owning packages are explicitly required.
No unsafe casts, local DTO mirrors, manual object readers/mappers, or trivial wrappers/helpers are approved by the revised implementation plan.
Implementation-improvement audit pass evidence is recorded before task acceptance.
completionCriteria: The spec or a dated appendix contains current sanitized OAuth findings.
Implementation is either unblocked by current evidence or explicitly stopped before hosted OAuth code begins.

evidence: Parent audit 1 - ownership/call graph: Reviewed the current package and app boundaries. @bundjil/codex-oauth still owns reusable profiles and the intentionally unsupported CodexOAuthClientLive; apps/codex-proxy remains the private HTTP boundary. This documentation-only tracer bullet does not introduce an OAuth client, redirect route, token transfer, or new provider call graph.

Parent audit 2 - implementation quality: Reviewed the sanitized research diff and local selected CLI evidence. The decision correctly refuses to emulate the fixed public CLI client or its localhost callback. No Effect program, schema, service, layer, DTO, helper, cast, raw token, or unsafe storage path was added; later implementation requirements remain intact.

Parent audit 3 - verification coverage: Parent confirmed the selected CLI login-status command exits successfully without exposing account output, rechecked all local binary versions, ran `jq empty`, `git diff --check`, `bun run check`, and `bun run verification`. The manual helper's missing integrity header and the supported-source fallback are recorded in the active plan.

tasks.title: Define encrypted profile schemas and cipher service
tasks.description: Add versioned encrypted profile contracts and a CodexOAuthProfileCipher service in @bundjil/codex-oauth.
implementationNotes: Define an `EncryptedCodexOAuthProfileV1` schema with version, algorithm, key id, nonce, ciphertext, subject hash, and timestamps.
Define schema-backed tagged errors for key config, encryption, decryption, decode, unsupported version, and key mismatch failures.
Use Effect Config with Redacted values for encryption key material and non-secret config for key id and algorithm.
Provide deterministic or memory-safe test layers and a WebCrypto-compatible live layer.
Prove encrypted stored values do not contain raw token fields or OAuth payload fragments.
Keep JSON string boundaries on Effect Schema codecs.
mandatoryVerification: Flat meaningful Effect.gen programs for encrypt and decrypt operations.
Typed errors handled in .pipe(...) with catchTag, catchTags, or mapError.
Canonical Effect Schema contracts and schema-derived types from @bundjil/codex-oauth.
No unsafe casts, local DTO mirrors, manual object readers/mappers, or trivial wrappers/helpers.
Tests cover round trip, wrong key, wrong key id, malformed ciphertext, unsupported version, and leak checks.
bun run --filter @bundjil/codex-oauth test
bun run --filter @bundjil/codex-oauth check-types
completionCriteria: @bundjil/codex-oauth exports encrypted profile schemas, cipher service tag, live/test layers, and safe errors.
No hosted storage layer composes raw token profiles after this task.

evidence: Parent audit 1 - ownership/call graph: Kept encryption contracts, config service, cipher service, errors, layers, exports, tests, and package documentation inside @bundjil/codex-oauth. CodexProfileStore remains raw/local-only in this task; hosted encrypted persistence, locks, OAuth routes, proxy live mode, and apps/agent remain unchanged.

Parent audit 2 - Effect implementation quality: Reviewed versioned Effect Schema envelopes, Context.Service tags, explicit live/test layers, Config.redacted key material, flat named Effect.fn generator operations, typed tagged cipher errors, and Schema.fromJsonString profile/envelope codecs. Redacted.value appears only immediately before base64 decode and WebCrypto key import. Scans found no JSON.stringify, process.env, Object.values/Object.entries, switch branches, unsafe casts, DTO mirrors, or trivial boundary wrappers in the new cipher path.

Parent audit 3 - verification coverage: Tests cover encryption/decryption round trip, no token/scope leakage from a schema-encoded envelope, wrong encryption key, wrong key id, malformed ciphertext, unsupported version, missing config, and the live Config/WebCrypto composition. Parent passed package check-types, 33 package tests, package build, jq validation, git diff check, and full bun run verification.

tasks.title: Implement encrypted storage and refresh locking
tasks.description: Compose CodexProfileStore over encrypted KeyValueStore persistence and add refresh locking for hosted rotation safety.
implementationNotes: Add `CodexProfileStoreEncryptedKeyValueLive` or an equivalent layer that encrypts before writing and decrypts after reading.
Keep the existing raw KeyValueStore profile layer available only for local tests and non-hosted proof.
Add `CodexOAuthRefreshLock` with lock acquisition, contention, expiry, and release errors.
Use an atomic Upstash lock operation when hosted; keep Redis command details behind a package-owned service/layer.
Use the existing canonical subject-hash derivation for profile and lock keys.
Test concurrent refresh programs so only one invocation refreshes while followers retry or read the updated profile.
mandatoryVerification: Flat meaningful Effect.gen programs for store read/write/delete and lock with-refresh operations.
Typed errors handled in .pipe(...) with catchTag, catchTags, or mapError.
Canonical Effect Schema contracts and schema-derived types from @bundjil/codex-oauth.
No unsafe casts, local DTO mirrors, manual object readers/mappers, or trivial wrappers/helpers.
Tests cover encrypted save/read/remove, Upstash-like adapter behavior, lock contention, lock expiry, owner-only release, refresh race behavior, and leak checks.
bun run --filter @bundjil/codex-oauth test
bun run --filter @bundjil/codex-oauth check-types
bun run --filter @bundjil/codex-oauth build
completionCriteria: Hosted profile persistence can be composed through encrypted storage over Effect KeyValueStore.
Refresh coordination has a package-owned service contract and test evidence.

evidence: Parent audit 1 - ownership/call graph: package-only encrypted persistence and refresh coordination; apps, routes, and deployment remain unchanged.

Parent audit 2 - Effect implementation quality: verified Context services, explicit layers, Schema persistence, tagged errors, atomic Upstash acquire/release, and lock-aware refreshAccessToken re-read behavior.

Parent audit 3 - verification coverage: encrypted-store leak checks, lock contention/expiry/owner release, concurrency coordination, Upstash adapter behavior, package check-types/tests/build, and full bun run verification passed.

tasks.title: Implement live OAuth client and proxy routes
tasks.description: Add live Codex OAuth endpoint exchange in @bundjil/codex-oauth and private operator OAuth routes in apps/codex-proxy.
implementationNotes: Add `CodexOAuthClientLive` only after the OAuth surface is revalidated.
Add `CodexOAuthStateStore` for short-lived PKCE verifier and state values.
Expose private/operator `GET /codex/oauth/start`, `GET /codex/oauth/callback`, and `POST /codex/oauth/logout` routes in apps/codex-proxy.
Guard OAuth routes with deployment protection, an internal admin token, or another explicit operator-only mechanism.
Callback output must be minimal and must not include token values, auth codes, raw OAuth payloads, prompts, or cookies.
State and callback payload handling must use canonical schemas and typed tagged errors.
mandatoryVerification: Flat meaningful Effect.gen programs for start, callback, exchange, state validation, profile save, and logout operations.
Typed errors handled in .pipe(...) with catchTag, catchTags, or mapError.
Canonical Effect Schema contracts and schema-derived types from @bundjil/codex-oauth and apps/codex-proxy config.
No unsafe casts, local DTO mirrors, manual object readers/mappers, or trivial wrappers/helpers.
Tests cover start redirect, state storage, callback state mismatch, exchange failure, encrypted profile save, logout deletion, route auth failures, and safe output.
bun run --filter @bundjil/codex-oauth test
bun run --filter @bundjil/codex-proxy test
bun run --filter @bundjil/codex-proxy check-types
completionCriteria: A local/mock OAuth route flow can store an encrypted profile without live secrets.
Live OAuth route code remains disabled or unconfigured until Vercel preview env is ready.

tasks.title: Wire live Codex proxy mode
tasks.description: Compose apps/codex-proxy live mode with encrypted storage, refresh locking, and direct Codex Responses calls.
implementationNotes: Keep `BUNDJIL_CODEX_PROXY_MODE=mock` as the default local proof path.
Allow `BUNDJIL_CODEX_PROXY_MODE=live` only when required encrypted storage, OAuth, subject, and internal-token config is present.
Use Effect Config and ConfigProvider.fromEnv through app-owned config files.
Do not read platform API keys as fallback credentials in subscription mode.
Keep apps/agent unchanged except for docs/config examples unless separate implementation work is explicitly approved.
Route diagnostics must be sanitized and schema-backed.
mandatoryVerification: Flat meaningful Effect.gen programs for live route handling, profile resolution, refresh, provider streaming, and route error mapping.
Typed errors handled in .pipe(...) with catchTag, catchTags, or mapError.
Canonical Effect Schema contracts and schema-derived types from owning packages.
No unsafe casts, local DTO mirrors, manual object readers/mappers, or trivial wrappers/helpers.
Tests cover missing live config, encrypted profile missing, refresh-required flow, refresh failure, Codex HTTP failure, streaming success, and no API-key fallback.
bun run --filter @bundjil/codex-proxy test
bun run --filter @bundjil/codex-proxy check-types
bun run --filter @bundjil/codex-proxy build

Mock mode remains working and is still safe for preview smoke tests.

Keep production env unset until preview live proof passes and production is explicitly approved.

Do not deploy production unless the user explicitly approves after preview proof.

tasks.title: Run 3-pass Effect TS audit sweep
tasks.description: Run a dedicated cross-cutting 3-pass Effect TS audit sweep across the completed hosted Codex implementation before any production decision.
implementationNotes: Pass 1 is the ownership and call-graph sweep: verify `@bundjil/codex-oauth`, `apps/codex-proxy`, and `apps/agent` own only their intended behavior and that production, test, CLI/script, Vercel, and rollback call graphs match the spec.

Pass 2 is the Effect implementation-quality sweep: inspect the diff for flat primary Effect.gen or Effect.fn programs, Context.Service tags, explicit Layers, Schema-derived types, package-owned tagged errors, Config.redacted, ConfigProvider.fromEnv, Redacted handling only at final provider boundaries, schema-backed KeyValueStore persistence, and no unsafe casts, DTO mirrors, manual object readers, process.env package reads, switch/case branching, instanceof checks, or helper sprawl.

Pass 3 is the verification-coverage sweep: prove targeted package/app commands, root verification, local mock proof, hosted preview OAuth proof, hosted preview live model proof, Upstash encrypted readback, Vercel log/leak scans, docs updates, rollback notes, and any skipped production proof with reason.
If any pass finds a gap, fix it and record another pass. Do not treat three entries as sufficient when an unresolved issue remains.
Production deploy requires a separate explicit user approval after this sweep.
mandatoryVerification: All prior tasks have completed audit evidence.
Ownership/call graph audit confirms `@bundjil/codex-oauth`, `apps/codex-proxy`, and `apps/agent` boundaries match the spec.

Implementation quality audit confirms flat primary Effect.gen or Effect.fn programs, Context.Service tags, schema-derived types, tagged errors, Config.redacted, ConfigProvider.fromEnv, explicit layers, Redacted handling only at final provider boundaries, schema-backed KeyValueStore persistence, no unsafe casts, no DTO mirrors, no manual object readers, and no helper sprawl.
Verification coverage audit records targeted checks, root verification, Vercel preview proof, log/leak scans, Upstash readback, docs updates, and any skipped production proof with reason.
The audit sweep itself is recorded in this task's implementationImprovementAudit evidence, not only in prior task notes.
bun run verification
git status --short is reviewed before commit
completionCriteria: The dedicated 3-pass audit sweep is complete with evidence for ownership/call graph, Effect implementation quality, and verification coverage.
Hosted live Codex mode is either complete in preview with production pending approval, or blocked with concrete evidence.
The task ledger and spec are updated to reflect final state.
Commit and push only after verification passes.
